title: Sleep apnea symptoms
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Supports clinician-reviewed respiratory polygraphy, home oximetry where appropriate, polysomnography in the specified context, and use of sleep-study results by clinicians to diagnose and grade OSAHS.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed sleep-study and oximetry fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-reviewed respiratory polygraphy, home oximetry where appropriate, polysomnography in the specified context, and use of sleep-study results by clinicians to diagnose and grade OSAHS.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

treatment_and_management.text: Supports tailored follow-up, CPAP review, annual review after optimisation, symptoms, Epworth score, vigilance and driving, AHI or ODI, adherence, treatment experience, sleep hygiene, and clinician-reviewed sleep-study follow-up.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports mapped arranged follow-up and ongoing symptoms, Epworth, vigilance, adherence, sleep-hygiene, and sleep-study review fields.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports tailored follow-up, CPAP review, annual review after optimisation, symptoms, Epworth score, vigilance and driving, AHI or ODI, adherence, treatment experience, sleep hygiene, and clinician-reviewed sleep-study follow-up.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

medication.text: Supports tailored clinician discussion of weight, smoking, alcohol, sleep hygiene, and CPAP in recommendation-qualified diagnosed OSAHS contexts; no medicine, device setting, or autonomous treatment output is mapped.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-discussed weight, sleep-hygiene, CPAP, and lifestyle fields only in recommendation-qualified OSAHS contexts.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports tailored clinician discussion of weight, smoking, alcohol, sleep hygiene, and CPAP in recommendation-qualified diagnosed OSAHS contexts; no medicine, device setting, or autonomous treatment output is mapped.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1 and 1.5.1–1.6.7 — lifestyle and treatment discussion

referral.text: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped adult symptom-profile, obesity context, Epworth, BMI, neck-circumference assessment context, presets, and structured history.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports snoring, witnessed apnoeas, unrefreshing or fragmented sleep, waking headache, sleepiness or fatigue, nocturia, choking, cognitive symptoms, obesity context, Epworth Sleepiness Scale, and STOP-Bang, while prohibiting use of Epworth alone to determine referral.

source.title: Obstructive sleep apnoea/hypopnoea syndrome and obesity hypoventilation syndrome in over 16s

referral.text: Supports referral information on assessment scores, functional sleepiness, comorbidities, occupational or driving risk, and oxygen saturation or blood gases if available.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped severe sleepiness, driving-risk, oxygen information, referral, and driving-discussion documentation.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports referral information on assessment scores, functional sleepiness, comorbidities, occupational or driving risk, and oxygen saturation or blood gases if available.

source_limitations: People aged 16 years or younger are outside the exact source.

Sleep breathing disorder alias, syncope, acute respiratory distress, nocturnal seizures, generic blood pressure or oropharyngeal examination, apnoea-with-desaturation and respiratory-failure red-flag labels, generic clinician plan, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some examination fields remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, severity, driving fitness, device selection, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
